rationale: Comprehensive UI rework across the three project files (`index.html`, `style.css`, `script.js`) matching every claim in the Portuguese PR body. Adds the scroll progress bar (`<div class="scroll-progress" id="scrollProgress">`) plus a grain overlay. Hero gains three `.orb` elements with `data-parallax` attributes, a `.grid-3d` background, a `.card-3d` containing the existing hero image with `card-3d-inner`/`card-3d-shine`, and two `.float-badge` overlays with parallax factors. Sections use `[data-reveal]` attributes (with optional `data-reveal-delay`) and cards/forms gain `.glass-3d` plus `[data-tilt]`. `script.js` implements the corresponding behavior: `var reduzirMovimento = window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)').matches;` guards animations, `document.querySelectorAll('[data-parallax]').forEach(...)` walks the parallax elements on scroll with per-element factor parsing, and an IntersectionObserver wires the reveal animations. The header gains a blur-on-scroll behavior and the theme button persists choice via localStorage as the body promises. Pure HTML/CSS/JS with no external libraries, consistent with the `sem frameworks` claim.
